content:
Hello!

I've been trying to test this patch: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20240820194910.187826-3-mmaurer@google.com/ from the rust-next tree. I've gotten cross compilation working, cross compiled a busybox initramfs, and have been trying to find a target that qemu can emulate MTE on.

Starting with the arm64 defconfig (enabling rust), compiling the kernel and busybox with make ARCH=arm64 MARCH=armv9-a CROSS_COMPILE=aarch64-linux-gnu-

Right now my qemu does not show a BIOS:

qemu-system-aarch64 \
        -m 2048 \
        -cpu cortex-a710 \
        -machine virt,mte=on \
        -kernel /home/gtn/linux/arch/arm64/boot/Image \
        -initrd /home/gtn/initrds/arm64v9a_initramfs.cpio.gz \
        -nographic \
        -display none \
        -append "console=ttyS0 loglevel=6 nokaslr" \

I think it's because I am missing the dts but this is my first time compiling for ARM and trying to test it via qemu

Is there a good target triple or dts that lets an x86 host emulate ARM with MTE on qemu?
